reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully formalizes Munkres Exercise 28.5 (Theorem 28.2). The `countably_compact` definition captures the standard notion: every countable open cover has a finite subcover. The RHS of the iff encodes exactly the informal claim: for every sequence of closed (`IsClosed (C n)`) nonempty (`C n ≠ ∅`) nested (`C (n+1) ⊆ C n`, matching $C_n \supset C_{n+1}$) sets, there is a point `x` in every `C n` (nonempty intersection). The iff direction matches "if and only if", the quantifier structure is correct, and no hypothesis is silently added or removed. The indexing from ℕ (starting at 0 vs 1) is an irrelevant reindexing that does not affect the mathematical content.
